rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO CO.** IN THE MATTER OF THE ROGER W. SLEIGHT 2010 DATED NOVEMBER 18, 2010 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena And Request For Protective Order Ccp 1987.1 And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. The Court grants co-trustee Mark C. Backer's Request for Judicial Notice. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (21015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Petitioners Objectors and Contingent Beneficiaries Zachary Sleight, Sharon Sleight and Joshua Sleight's ("Beneficiaries") and counsel Mark Malachowski's (collectively "Moving Parties") motion to quash deposition subpoena and request for protective order CCP 1987.1 and sanctions is DENIED. As for the motion to quash, Moving Parties have failed to show that a valid subpoena served or putatively served on Malachowski is pending; there is no legal basis for a so-called "preemptive motion to quash." As a separate and independent grounds for denying the motion, Moving Parties failed to file a meet and confer declaration under CCP section 2016.040 as required for motions to quash. (See CCP section 2025.410(c).) As for a protective order, Moving Parties have not shown that a valid subpoena served or putatively served on Malachowski is pending. A separate and independent grounds for denial of this relief is that Moving Parties failed to submit a declaration showing good faith efforts to resolve the issues informally (CCP section 2025.420(d)). Moving Parties' request for sanctions is DENIED The court finds that motion was without merit and, therefore, no grounds for sanctions in favor of Moving Parties exist under any authority. Co-trustee Mark C. Backer's request for sanctions under CCP section 2025.410(d) is DENIED. The court finds that circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. Moving Parties' motion was without merit; at the same time, Backer's persistent demand for discovery from Malachowski is unsupported by the record. Moving Parties oppose the subpoena, in part, because it is not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. In his opposition, Backer represents that he has evidence that "strongly suggests" that the Beneficiaries and co-trustee Marilyn Bessey agreed that the Beneficiaries would not object to Bessey's accounting in exchange for Bessy "us[ing] her position as Co-Trustee [sic] to help [the Beneficiaries'] efforts to defeat the Settlor's intent to create a support trust for [Backer]." While Backer has filed his own petition and several verified responses to other petitions, he has failed to identify any relevant allegation in any of the verified pleadings, let alone link such allegations to a pending request for relief available from this court. Independently, even if relevance were shown, there is no evidence Backer has tailored his unserved draft subpoena to account for the limitations on compelling an attorney to produce evidence related to his representation of opposing parties in the very action in which the discovery is sought. Co-Trustee Mark C. Backer is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on December 1, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate

rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required for the court to take a first look at the pleadings and discuss how it can help the parties resolve the issues. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 7th Amended General Order which took effect on 10/30/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/30/2020 (no earlier date available). 1) No hard copies of proposed orders have been received. 2) No proof of publication is on file. 3) Is Bond Services of California specified in item 1 of the petiton a newspaper of general circulation in San Francisco within the meaning of Probate Code section 8121(b)? 4) No proof of service of notice of the hearing is on file. 5) Item 3f(3) was checked to allege that the original will or codicil has been lost, but the "lost" box in the caption was not checked, and no Attachment 3f(3) was filed.
